title: Assembly Language
summary: 9618 AS assembly — instruction formats, registers, addressing modes, and trace tables for simple programs.
learningObjectives: Describe the purpose of assembly language.
Interpret simple assembly instructions (LDR, STR, ADD, CMP, BNE).
Trace register and memory changes step by step.

simpleExplanation.title: One step above machine code
simpleExplanation.summary: Assembly uses mnemonics for opcodes. Each instruction maps closely to CPU operations — load, store, arithmetic, branch.
steps: LDR R1, [address] — load from memory to register.
STR R1, [address] — store register to memory.
CMP R1, #n then BNE label — conditional branch.
Trace table: PC, registers, flags after each line.

sections.content: **Assembly language** (4.2) links to **CPU architecture** (4.1). Paper 1 sets **trace tables** — work line by line, do not skip flag updates.

sections.question: After CMP R1, #5 followed by BNE loop when R1 = 5, is branch taken?
sections.solution:
CMP sets flags comparing R1 − 5.

R1 = 5 → result zero → Z flag set.

BNE branches when **not equal** — Z clear.

**Branch not taken** — fall through to next instruction.
